zh.Waltham Abbey, Essex, England: 英國艾可沃爾坦 艾比

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster於1312年9月17日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡里克 卡素時，他的父親John de Burgh26歲，母親Elizabeth de Clare17歲。他於1327年5月1日與Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他於1331年在愛爾蘭被列為lieutenant of ireland。他去世於1333年6月6日在愛爾蘭貝爾法斯特享年20歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威阿騰利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster于1312年9月17日出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡里克 卡素时，他的父亲John de Burgh26岁，母亲Elizabeth de Clare17岁。他于1327年5月1日与Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。他于1331年在爱尔兰被列为lieutenant of ireland。他去世于1333年6月6日在爱尔兰貝爾法斯特享年20岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 高威阿騰利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Donn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ancient byname meaning either ‘brown’ or ‘chief, noble’, in use from the earliest times until the 19th century.

variants.name: Don
meanings: Short form of   Donald .  It is also a variant of the Irish name Donn .

en.lifeSummary: When William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster was born on 17 September 1312, in Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Ireland, his father, John de Burgh, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth de Clare, was 17. He married Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster on 1 May 1327, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. In 1331, his occupation is listed as lieutenant of ireland in Ireland. He died on 6 June 1333, in Belfast, Ireland, at the age of 20, and was buried in Athenry, County Galway, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster est né le 17 septembre 1312, à Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, John de Burgh, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Clare, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster le 1 mai 1327, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. En 1331, sa profession de son indiquée est lieutenant of ireland à Irlande. Il est décédé le 6 juin 1333, à Belfast, Irlande, à l’âge de 20 ans, et a été enterré à Athenry, County Galway, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster am 17. September 1312 in Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Burgh 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de Clare 17. Er hat am 1. Mai 1327 in England, United Kingdom Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. 1331 ist sein Beruf als lieutenant of ireland in Irland aufgeführt. Er starb am 6. Juni 1333 im Alter von 20 Jahren in Belfast, Irland und wurde in Athenry, County Galway, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster è nato il il 17 settembre 1312 nel Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Irlanda, il suo padre, John de Burgh, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de Clare, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster il 1 maggio 1327, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Nel 1331, la sua occupazione è indicata come lieutenant of ireland nel Irlanda. Morì il 6 giugno 1333, nel Belfast, Irlanda, all'età di 20 anni, e fu sepolto a Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulsterが1312年9月17日にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアカーリック カストルで生まれた時，父John de Burghは26歳，母Elizabeth de Clareは17歳であった。1327年5月1日にイギリスイギリスでMaud of Lancaster Countess of Ulsterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。1331年，アイルランドでの職業はlieutenant of irelandと記録されている。彼は1333年6月6日にアイルランドベルファストで20歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ガルワイアスエンリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster(이)가 1312년 9월 17일 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카릭 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Burgh(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Clare(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1327년 5월 1일 영국 잉글랜드에서 Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1331년, 그의 직업은 아일랜드에서 lieutenant of ireland이었다. 그는 1333년 6월 6일 아일랜드 벨파스트에서 20세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 앳헨리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster nasceu em 17 de setembro de 1312, em Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Irlanda, seu pai, John de Burgh, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Clare, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster em 1 de maio de 1327, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Em 1331, sua profissão aparece como lieutenant of ireland em Irlanda. Ele faleceu em 6 de junho de 1333, em Belfast, Irlanda, com 20 anos, e foi sepultado em Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster родился 17 сентября 1312 г. в местности каррикк кастл, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, его отцу по имени John de Burgh было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Clare, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster 1 мая 1327 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. В 1331 году он работал по профессии lieutenant of ireland в местности Ирландия. Он умер 6 июня 1333 г. в местности белфаст, Ирландия в возрасте 20 лет и был похоронен в местности афенри, коунти галвеи, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Donn de Burgh 3rd Earl of Ulster nació el 17 de septiembre de 1312, en Carrick Castle, County Kildare, Irlanda, su papá, John de Burgh, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Clare, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Maud of Lancaster Countess of Ulster el 1 de mayo de 1327, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. En 1331, su ocupación registrada era lieutenant of ireland en Irlanda. Murió el 6 de junio de 1333, en Belfast, Irlanda, a la edad de 20 años, y fue sepultado en Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.
